name: Vedanta Deshika
dates_notes: Sectarian chronicles plus manuscript colophons; dates well-established.

dates_evidence.description: Śrīvaiṣṇava sectarian chronicles document his birth, education, and demise dates.
Manuscript colophons across his many works confirm his floruit and lineage.

lineage_polemical.note: The Vaḍakalai-Tenkalai split formally crystallizes around Vedānta Deśika's monkey-hold (markaṭa-nyāya) account vs. Pillai-Lokācārya's cat-hold (mārjāra-nyāya).

core_thesis: Vedānta Deśika is the systematiser, logician, and Vaḍakalai polemicist of post-Rāmānuja Viśiṣṭādvaita. He preserves the school's core architecture — Brahman as Nārāyaṇa-with-Śrī, qualified by *cit* and *acit* as his body in *apṛthak-siddha* relation — and extends it on three fronts. (i) He completes the school's engagement with Navya-Nyāya logic and Nyāya-Vaiśeṣika categoriology, building (in the *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* and *Nyāya-Siddhāñjana*) a Viśiṣṭādvaita-compatible *padārtha*-scheme and *pramāṇa*-theory that can stand against Gaṅgeśa's school. (ii) He produces in the *Śatadūṣaṇī* the most systematic anti-Advaita polemic the tradition possesses — one hundred *dūṣaṇa*-s targeting every load-bearing claim of post-Śaṅkara Advaita. (iii) On the *upāya*-question he defends the Vaḍakalai position: *bhakti-yoga* requires the surrendered self's continuing effort and qualifications, and *prapatti* itself is structured by the six *aṅga*-s as conditions to be enacted; grace cooperates with the agent's continuing co-action — the *markaṭa-nyāya* (monkey-baby that grips its mother) image, against the Tenkalai *mārjāra-nyāya* (kitten that the mother cat carries). He retains and defends Sanskritic Vedic learning as essential to the school.

engaged_works.title: Tattva-Mukta-Kalapa with Sarvartha-Siddhi
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored Viśiṣṭādvaita systematic verse-treatise with auto-commentary.
engaged_works.summary: A 500-verse Sanskrit *kārikā*-treatise with the author's own prose auto-commentary *Sarvārtha-Siddhi*, organised by the Viśiṣṭādvaita ontology of *dravya* and *adravya* into *sara*-chapters on *jaḍa* (matter), *jīva* (self), *Īśvara*, *nitya-vibhūti* (the eternal divine realm), *dharma-bhūta-jñāna*, and so on. The *Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa* is the school's mature systematic ontology: it argues each Viśiṣṭādvaita category against the alternatives — Sāṃkhya *prakṛti*, Naiyāyika *paramāṇu*-atomism, Buddhist momentariness, Advaitic *māyā*, Bhāskara's *aupādhika-bheda*, Madhva's unqualified *bheda* — fixing precisely how the school's *apṛthak-siddha* relation differs from each. The *Sarvārtha-Siddhi* is unusually rigorous in its handling of *dharmi-bhūta-jñāna* (substance-awareness as itself the *jīva*'s constitutive attribute) and of the *śarīra-śarīrin* relation as a sui generis ontological relation distinct from inherence (*samavāya*) and from mere conjunction. The work matters because it is the canonical reference for Vaḍakalai metaphysics — the *Tattva-Mukta-Kalāpa* with *Sarvārtha-Siddhi* is what later Viśiṣṭādvaita scholasticism quotes when fixing a category-boundary.

engaged_works.title: Rahasya-Traya-Sara
engaged_works.summary: A long Tamil-Maṇipravāḷa rahasya-treatise in 32 *adhikāra*-s expounding the three Śrīvaiṣṇava esoteric *mantra*-s (*tirumantra* / *aṣṭākṣara*, *dvaya*, *carama-śloka*) and through them the totality of Vaḍakalai soteriology. The *Rahasya-Traya-Sāra* is the canonical Vaḍakalai handling of the *upāya*-question: *prapatti* (*śaraṇāgati*) is enjoined for those incapable of sustained *bhakti-yoga* and is structured by the six *aṅga*-s as conditions the surrendered self must satisfy; the *jīva* retains real agency (*kartṛtva*) under the Lord's permission (*anumati*), and grace presupposes the surrendered self's continuing co-action — the *markaṭa-nyāya* image. Deśika carefully discriminates *prapatti* from *bhakti-yoga* without making them rivals, and articulates Śrī's role as *puruṣakāra* (mediating advocate) rather than as an independent *upāya*. The work matters because it is the polemical and pedagogical anchor of the entire Vaḍakalai school: every later Vaḍakalai position on grace, agency, qualification, and the status of Śrī cites the *Rahasya-Traya-Sāra* as authority. Composed in Maṇipravāḷa precisely to be accessible to non-Sanskritist Śrīvaiṣṇavas without ceding the field to the Tenkalai.

engaged_works.title: Pancharatra-Raksha
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored defense of Pāñcarātra ritual practice.
engaged_works.summary: Sanskrit polemical *prakaraṇa* defending the *prāmāṇya* of the Pāñcarātra *saṃhitā*-s and the legitimacy of Vaiṣṇava temple-ritual, extending Yāmuna's *Āgama-Prāmāṇya* against later — especially Smārta and Advaita-aligned — challenges that resurfaced in the late-medieval period. Deśika argues *sūtra*-by-*sūtra* through the *Brahma-Sūtra*'s *Utpatti-Asambhava-Adhikaraṇa* (2.2.42–45) — the *adhikaraṇa* on which the orthodoxy of Pāñcarātra hinges — to show that, properly read, the *sūtra*-s do not refute Pāñcarātra cosmology but only one heterodox version of *vyūha*-doctrine, leaving the orthodox core intact and authoritative. He also argues for the equivalence of Pāñcarātra-initiates to Vedic *brāhmaṇa*-s for ritual purposes and defends specific Pāñcarātra liturgical elements (*pañca-saṃskāra*, image-installation, *mudrā*-s) against *Mīmāṃsā* objections. The work matters because it secured the institutional authority of the Śrīvaiṣṇava temple system — particularly Śrīraṅgam, Tirupati, and Kāñcī — at the precise moment when sectarian competition with Advaita-aligned Smārta institutions was intensifying.

engaged_works.title: Nyaya-Parishuddhi
engaged_works.summary: Comprehensive Viśiṣṭādvaita *pramāṇa*-treatise — the school's mature engagement with Nyāya logic and epistemology and its definitive *prāmāṇya*-theory. Organised around the three *pramāṇa*-s of Viśiṣṭādvaita (*pratyakṣa*, *anumāna*, *śabda* — *upamāna* and *arthāpatti* are reduced to *anumāna*; *anupalabdhi* is rejected as redundant), the *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* defends *yathārtha-khyāti* (all cognition as veridical, error being only partial apprehension) against the *anyathā-* and *anirvacanīya-khyāti* alternatives; defends the *svataḥ-prāmāṇya* of *śabda*; and constructs a Viśiṣṭādvaita-compatible theory of *vyāpti* and *paramārśa* that engages directly with the early Navya-Nyāya of Gaṅgeśa. The work matters because before Deśika the school had Yāmuna's *Saṃvit-Siddhi* and Rāmānuja's scattered epistemological remarks but no comprehensive *pramāṇa*-treatise; the *Nyāya-Pariśuddhi* fills that gap and gives Viśiṣṭādvaita the technical resources to argue on equal logical footing with the dominant Naiyāyika and Advaita scholasticisms of the late thirteenth and fourteenth centuries.

engaged_works.title: Nyaya-Siddhanjana
engaged_works.summary: Systematic Viśiṣṭādvaita *prakaraṇa* on the seven categories — *dravya* (substance), *guṇa*, *karma*, *sāmānya*, *viśeṣa*, *samavāya*, *abhāva* — re-engineered against the Nyāya-Vaiśeṣika scheme to fit Viśiṣṭādvaita ontology. Substance is divided into *jaḍa* (*prakṛti*, *kāla*, *śuddha-sattva* of the eternal realm) and *ajaḍa* (*jīva*, *Īśvara*, *dharma-bhūta-jñāna*); the *jīva* is the substrate of its own attribute-awareness *dharma-bhūta-jñāna*, which expands and contracts under *karma* but is, in its essential nature, infinite; in *mokṣa* it expands to encompass Brahman and the eternal realm without merging the *jīva* into Brahman. Deśika here gives the most precise analytic statement of *apṛthak-siddhi* as a relation distinct from both *samavāya* (inherence) and conjunction (*saṃyoga*), and clarifies how *Īśvara* can be the *antaryāmin* of *jīva*-s and *acit* without being reducible to or identical with them. The work matters because it is the school's mature *padārtha*-systematisation, complementing the *Tattva-Mukta-Kalāpa* and providing the technical vocabulary used by Sudarśana Sūri's *Śruta-Prakāśikā* and the later commentarial tradition.

engaged_works.title: Shata-Dushani
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored anti-Advaita polemic in 100 dūṣaṇa-s.
engaged_works.summary: *One hundred refutations of Advaita* — the most systematic anti-Advaita polemic of the entire Viśiṣṭādvaita tradition. The *Śatadūṣaṇī* targets every load-bearing thesis of post-Śaṅkara Advaita as developed by Maṇḍana, Sureśvara, Vācaspati, Padmapāda, Prakāśātman, Sarvajñātman, and Śrīharṣa: the doctrine of *avidyā* (extending and sharpening Rāmānuja's *sapta-vidhā-anupapatti*); the *anirvacanīya-khyāti* theory of error; the *dṛṣṭi-sṛṣṭi* and *sṛṣṭi-dṛṣṭi* cosmologies; the doctrine of *nirviśeṣa* Brahman; the Advaitic readings of *tat tvam asi* and *neti neti*; the *bimba-pratibimba* and *avaccheda* models of the *jīva*; the *māyā/avidyā* distinction in later Advaita; the use of *jahad-ajahal-lakṣaṇā*; and Śrīharṣa's *khaṇḍana*-style attacks on definitions. Against Śrīharṣa's anti-definitional moves Deśika reasserts that a coherent *padārtha*-scheme is possible and presupposed by every dialectical exchange. The work matters because it is the high-water mark of Vaḍakalai polemic and because every later Viśiṣṭādvaita engagement with Advaita — through Mahācārya, Śrīnivāsadāsa, and into the modern period — operates within the framework of objections the *Śatadūṣaṇī* established. Deśika did not finish the planned hundred *dūṣaṇa*-s in his lifetime; the tradition completed the count.

engaged_works.title: Adhikarana-Saravali
engaged_works.composition_dates_notes: Composed in Deśika's mature period; companion to the Tattva-Mukta-Kalāpa as a Brahma-Sūtra-organized doctrinal summary.
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored verse-summary of the Brahma-Sūtra adhikaraṇas in Vaḍakalai Viśiṣṭādvaita perspective.
engaged_works.summary: A verse-summary (*sārāvalī*, "flow of essence") of the *Brahma-Sūtra*'s 156 *adhikaraṇas*, organized sequentially through the four *adhyāyas* and giving for each *adhikaraṇa* the Vaḍakalai Viśiṣṭādvaita reading in compressed *anuṣṭubh* form. For each *adhikaraṇa* Deśika supplies the *viṣaya* (topic), the *saṃśaya* (doubt), the *pūrvapakṣa* (typically Advaita or Bhāskara), the *siddhānta* (the Rāmānuja-line conclusion as developed through Sudarśana Sūri's *Śruta-Prakāśikā*), and the *prayojana*. The work is the canonical Vaḍakalai pedagogical summary of the entire *Brahma-Sūtra* — what every Vaḍakalai pandit has memorized and what every Vaḍakalai composition presupposes. Doctrinally distinctive readings include the *ānanda-mayādhikaraṇa* (taking *ānandamaya* as Brahman, against Śaṅkara), the *jīva-pratibimbādhikaraṇa* (where Deśika fixes the *apṛthak-siddha* relation against the Advaitic *avaccheda* and Bhāskara's *aupādhika-bheda*), and the *adhyāya*-4 *bheda-sūtras* (read in *taratamya*-key consistent with Viśiṣṭādvaita's *jīva*-individuation). The work is the natural counterpart to the *Tattva-Mukta-Kalāpa*, reorganizing the same doctrinal architecture along the *Sūtra*'s own *adhikaraṇa*-skeleton.

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
cāturvyūhaṃ paraṃ brahma ṣāḍguṇya-parikarmitam |
pañcarātrasya kṛtsnasya prasūtiṃ paryupāsmahe ||

samasa_vigrahas.note: describes Brahman as manifested in the four vyūhas
qualified by the classical six divine excellences
traditional title of the āgama corpus

karaka_structure.note: appositional accusative: the source/origin of the Pāñcarātra as a whole

verb_modality.note: 1st person plural present indicative with devotional-reflexive force

key_passages.english_close: We reverentially worship the source of the whole Pāñcarātra: the supreme Brahman manifested as the four vyūhas and adorned with the six divine excellences.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: (emanational form of Viṣṇu such as Vāsudeva, Saṅkarṣaṇa, Pradyumna, Aniruddha)
(the six divine attributes, especially jñāna, bala, aiśvarya, śakti, vīrya, tejas)

key_passages.why_this_passage: This is not merely a conventional benediction: it states the positive theological premise of the whole work, namely that the Pāñcarātra issues from the supreme four-vyūha Brahman endowed with determinate auspicious qualities. Because the assigned Tattva-Muktā-Kalāpa scan was too OCR-degraded for trustworthy grammatical extraction, this clean Sanskrit opening is an access-constrained but fair way to represent Deśika's positive ontology from the assigned corpus.
key_passages.source_edition: Sri Pancaratra Raksa of Sri Vedanta Desika, critically edited by Pandit M. Duraiswami Aiyangar and Pandit T. Venugopalacharya, Adyar Library, Adyar, Madras, 1942.

key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
āgamasiddhānto mantrasiddhāntas tantrasiddhāntas tantrāntarasiddhānta iti |
catvāraś cete siddhāntā ṛgādivad eva śākhābhedair avantaratantrair bhidyante ||

pada_analysis.gloss: like the Ṛg and the others

samasa_vigrahas.note: indeclinable comparison: like the Ṛg and the rest

karaka_structure.note: further means or basis of subdivision

key_passages.english_close: "Āgama-siddhānta," "Mantra-siddhānta," "Tantra-siddhānta," and "Tantrāntara-siddhānta" thus: these doctrinal forms are four, and, like the Ṛg and the other Vedic recensions, are divided by branch-distinctions and subsidiary tantras.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: (a settled doctrinal formation or established interpretive line)
(a scriptural/ritual manual within the āgamic tradition)

key_passages.why_this_passage: This passage is representative because Deśika is not only defending one text but classifying the whole Pāñcarātra corpus as an internally articulated, Veda-analogous scriptural system. That taxonomic move is central to his epistemology of āgama and to the institutional claim that Śrīvaiṣṇava temple practice rests on a disciplined canon rather than on free sectarian invention.
key_passages.source_edition: Sri Pancaratra Raksa of Sri Vedanta Desika, critically edited by Pandit M. Duraiswami Aiyangar and Pandit T. Venugopalacharya, Adyar Library, Adyar, Madras, 1942.

key_passages.locus_long: Pāñcarātra-Rakṣā, prathamo'dhikāraḥ, section on Vaikhānasa-Pāñcarātra non-conflict
key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
avirodhopayogābhyām ubhayor api śāstrayoḥ |
madhyasthair hi kṛto dharmo vidyāsthāna-niveśanam ||

pada_analysis.gloss: the assigning to proper domains of learning

samasa_vigrahas.note: those standing in the middle, i.e. impartial adjudicators
placing each within its proper disciplinary location

karaka_structure.note: genitive relation: the two scriptures whose non-conflict/usefulness is at issue
grounds on which the settlement is made
agent in instrumental with passive kṛtaḥ
subject-predicate core: the norm/settlement has been made

key_passages.english_close: Because of the non-conflict and usefulness of both scriptures, a normative settlement has indeed been made by impartial authorities: namely, the assigning of each to its proper domain of learning.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: (here: the normative rule or settlement, not merely religion)

key_passages.why_this_passage: This couplet fairly represents Deśika because his defense of Pāñcarātra is not a rejection of Vedic or Vaikhānasa practice but a carefully delimited account of compatibility, jurisdiction, and ritual propriety. It therefore shows the institutional and devotional side of his thought, where scriptural reasoning issues in ordered worship rather than in a merely abstract canon-theory.
key_passages.source_edition: Sri Pancaratra Raksa of Sri Vedanta Desika, critically edited by Pandit M. Duraiswami Aiyangar and Pandit T. Venugopalacharya, Adyar Library, Adyar, Madras, 1942.
